title: Editor.setPointInSelectedWindow(p)
detail: Replace 4 windowLayout = setWindowLeafPoint(...) callsites. Then make windowLayout/selectedWindowId private-writable.

title: Delete dead Keymap.pending/feed/clearPending
detail: KeymapStack is the only stateful dispatcher. Drop the no-op editor.keymap.clearPending() in keyboard-quit too.

title: defgeneric/defmethod for per-mode dispatch
detail: defgeneric(name); defmethod(name, mode, fn). Convert Mode.completeAtPoint/indentLine to methods. Tree-sitter forward-sexp becomes a method.
REDO: prior edit to plugins/magit/index.ts was overwritten (still has diffsByFile not parseDiff). Test file deleted.

title: BufferModel.lineStarts: incremental line index maintained in _splice — closes Perf-0, Perf-4, Perf-5
detail: [architect REFACTOR verdict; closes Perf-0, Perf-4, Perf-5] Add `lineStarts: number[]` (sorted offsets of line beginnings) to BufferModel, incrementally maintained inside the existing `_splice` funnel (buffer.ts:78-94). bsearch a/b in lineStarts, count newlines in removed/repl, splice the affected slice, shift tail by repl.length-(b-a). ~25 lines. Closes 3 findings rooted in 'no precomputed line boundaries → full text.split on hot paths': Perf-0 [critical] 7 per-render slice+split sites (lineCol, moveLine, visibleTextRegionFromStart, build-display-model, click-to-point, buffer-view, line-numbers firstVisibleLineNumber) → bsearch O(log L) / index arithmetic / slice with no split. Perf-4 [warning] line-numbers.ts mapVisibleOffset re-splits 2× per span → bsearch on sliced lineStarts. Perf-5 [warning] lsp/positions.ts positionToPoint full split 2× per diagnostic → lineStarts[line]+clamp(char) O(1). Also unblocks Perf-1 (tree-sitter tree.edit needs {row,column}).

title: windowLayout is public-writable; 5 non-kernel sites assign through it
detail: [api--0] windowLayout is NOT private — public writable field, 5 non-kernel call sites assign through it: lsp/navigation.ts:39,43, xref/jump.ts:13,21, xref/history.ts:48 all do `editor.windowLayout = setWindowLeafPoint(...)`. avy/index.ts:36, windmove/index.ts:81, build-display-model.ts:38,145 read it. Plugins are directly rewriting the kernel's window tree, bypassing persistSelectedWindowPoint/restoreSelectedWindowPoint invariants. Fix: Make `windowLayout` private with a `get layout(): WindowNode` readonly accessor for renderers/avy/windmove. Add kernel primitives `setSelectedWindowPoint(point)` and `setSelectedWindowStartLine(line)` and migrate the 5 assignment sites.

title: Three overlapping key-binding APIs with inconsistent registry keys (key/defineKey/applyKeyBinding)
detail: [api--1] `editor.key(seq,cmd)` (20 callers) == `defineKey('global',seq,cmd)`. `defineKey` normalizes to 'global-map'/'minibuffer-local-map' but STRIPS `-map` for mode maps. `applyKeyBinding` accepts 'global' OR 'global-map' and passes raw to `registerKeyBinding`, so `bindingKey()` produces different map keys depending on call path. `getKeyBinding('global','C-c h')` and `getKeyBinding('global-map','C-c h')` can differ; describe-key/restore-key operate on the wrong entry. Fix: Collapse to one canonical entry point: keep `defineKey(map,seq,cmd)`, normalize ALL map names to `-map` suffix form. Delete `editor.key()` (rewrite 20 callers to `defineKey('global-map',...)`) or make it a `@deprecated` forwarder. Rewrite `applyKeyBinding` to delegate entirely to `editor.defineKey`.

title: ~22 1:1-wrapped Editor methods are commands wearing method clothes
detail: [api--2] splitWindowBelow/Right, deleteOtherWindows, deleteWindow, nextWindow, next/previousBuffer, newTab/switchTab/closeTab, cycleTilingLayout, recenterTopBottom, scrollScreen, scrollOtherWindow, universalArgument, windowConfigurationToRegister, jumpToRegister, setSelectedWindowDedicated, recoverThisFile, minibufferPrevious/NextHistory/Complete/Submit/Cancel — each calls `this.changed('<cmd>')`. DESIGN.md targets <400 lines and 'defines no commands'; editor.ts is at 1294. Fix: Move bodies into lisp/window-cmds.ts, lisp/minibuf.ts, lisp/isearch-ui.ts, lisp/tab-bar.ts as command functions calling kernel primitives. Methods called as primitives by plugins (splitWindowBelow from magit:389, killBuffer, enable/disable/toggleMinorMode) stay; rest go. Delete wrapper definitions in commands.ts:529-538 and emacs-standard.ts:127-128.

title: Dead state: tilingLayout field + cycleTilingLayout() — written, read by nothing
detail: [api--3] Field written by the method, read by nothing — no display code, no host, no plugin consults it (rg confirms only self-reference + kernel.test.ts:751 + commands.ts:538 wrapper). The `tiling-cycle` command cycles a string into the void. Fix: Delete editor.ts:120, editor.ts:972-977, core/commands.ts:538, test/kernel.test.ts:751.

title: showLineNumbers() is a class method that linum-mode reassigns at runtime
detail: [api--4] Method-as-mutable-slot: kernel hardcodes knowledge of linum-mode AND a mode replaces a class method by instance assignment, defeating the type contract. Single consumer: build-display-model.ts:152. Fix: Delete `showLineNumbers` from Editor. Have linum-mode export `linumActiveFor(editor,buffer)` and call from build-display-model.ts:152, OR use a buffer-local `display-line-numbers` that linum-mode sets — Emacs-shaped, zero kernel surface.

title: Legacy compat getters with misleading types: get windows():string[] returns BUFFER ids; get selectedWindow():number
detail: [api--5] Predate the WindowNode tree; collide confusingly with windowLayout/selectedWindowId. Only one production caller (commands.ts:526 `editor.windows.length`); rest are tests. Fix: Delete both. Replace commands.ts:526 with `listWindowLeaves(editor.windowLayout).length === 1`. Migrate the 5 test files to assert against `editor.selectedWindowId` / `listWindowLeaves(...)`.

title: editor.addHook/removeHook are 1:1 forwarders to module-global hooks; misleadingly per-instance
detail: [api--6] Hooks registry is process-global, not per-Editor. Half the codebase imports from kernel/hooks directly; other half goes through wrapper. `captureCallerSource(3)` depth-fragile across the two paths. Fix: Delete addHook/removeHook from Editor; keep runHook (injects this as ctx.editor). Migrate save-hooks/subword/persist/lean4/install-hooks to import {addHook} or to disposable ctx.hook() from plugin-context.

title: consumePrefixArgument() is public with zero external callers
detail: [api--7] Only used internally by run() :601 and handleIsearchKey :1067. Exposing it invites plugins to consume the prefix arg out from under the dispatch loop. Fix: Make it `private`. Plugins already receive prefixArgument via CommandContext.

title: Top-level command catch dumps error.stack into the one-line echo area
detail: [erro-0] run-core.ts:41 `editor.message(error instanceof Error ? error.stack ?? error.message : String(error))`. Every uncaught throw (read-only buffer, ENOENT, LSP RPC fail, eval error) reaches the user as a multi-line V8 stack trace crammed into a one-line echo widget; same multi-KB blob appended to *messages*. Fix: Show error.message in echo area; write .stack only to *messages* (or gate on a `debug-on-error` custom var).

title: lsp-rename applyWorkspaceEdit: no per-file catch, no finally — half-renamed codebase on partial failure
detail: [erro-1] Loops `await editor.openFile(path); applyTextEdits(...)` over every file in the WorkspaceEdit with no per-iteration catch and no `finally`. If file N's openFile throws (EACCES, dangling symlink), files 1..N-1 are already mutated and dirty, currentBuffer left at N-1, switchToBuffer(originBufferId) restore at L78 never runs. Fix: Wrap loop body in try/catch accumulating failures; move origin-buffer restore into `finally`. Report `Renamed N of M files (K failed: …)`.

title: isearch fallthrough: throwing command leaves isearch stuck active
detail: [erro-2] When isearch active and key isn't printable/RET/DEL, handleIsearchKey returns null and dispatch falls through to `await this.run(fed.command,...)` at L640 with isearch still set. If that command throws, this.isearch never cleared; next printable key silently appends to search string instead of self-inserting. Fix: Before running fallthrough command, call `this.endIsearch()` (Emacs: any non-isearch command exits search), or wrap L640 run() in try/finally{endIsearch()}.

title: overridingTerminalLocalMap not cleared on pty exit when current buffer ≠ term — editor soft-locks
detail: [erro-3] Editor-global override; only teardown is term-line-mode, and pty exit handler runs it only if editor.currentBuffer===buffer. Mouse click into another window switches currentBuffer away while char-mode on; when shell later exits the override is never cleared. Every key resolves to term-send-raw → 'No term session'; C-c C-j is itself swallowed. Editor soft-locked until restart. Fix: In onExit handler, unconditionally clear overridingTerminalLocalMap if it is termRawMap (regardless of currentBuffer). Make term-line-mode clear override even when !sessions.has(buffer). Add keyboard-quit reset of overridingTerminalLocalMap=null as universal escape.

title: runHooks has no per-hook isolation; one throwing hook aborts the rest + skips changed()
detail: [erro-4] `for (const fn of getHooks(name)) await fn(ctx)` — single throwing post-command-hook aborts remaining hooks AND skips changed(). Throwing before-save-hook (formatter crashing on malformed input) aborts buffer.save() half-run; for save-some-buffers (no try/catch) aborts the whole multi-file save loop with stack trace. Fix: Wrap each `await fn(ctx)` in try/catch; log to *messages* and continue (Emacs run-hooks + condition-case semantics). Separately wrap each b.save() in save-some-buffers.

title: dired multi-file copy/rename: no per-entry catch — partial failure leaves listing lying about disk
detail: [erro-5] Multi-file copy/move loops `await cp(...)` / `await rename(...)` with no per-entry catch. EXDEV/EACCES on entry K aborts: entries 1..K-1 already moved, refreshDiredBuffer never runs so listing shows old names, user gets raw stack trace. Fix: Per-iteration try/catch accumulating {entry,err}; in finally always `await refreshDiredBuffer(buffer)` and message `Copied N (K failed: …)`. Same pattern wdired-finish-edit uses.

title: prompt(): minibufferDepth++ before executor; throw inside leaks depth and *Minibuffer-N* buffer
detail: [erro-6] minibufferDepth++ (L669) before Promise executor; only decrement is inside resolve (L689). If enterMode/addBuffer throws inside executor, Promise rejects but minibufferDepth stays +1 permanently and *Minibuffer-N* leaks. keyboard-quit checks editor.minibuffer (still null/prev) so can't fix; recursive-minibuffer guards see phantom level forever. Fix: Move minibufferDepth++ inside executor right before `this.minibuffer={...}`; wrap L671-694 in try/catch that on throw deletes the buffer, restores previous, decrements, rethrows.

title: eval-region/eval-expression: unwrapped evaluator.eval → full stack trace in echo area
detail: [erro-7] `await evaluator.eval(code)` unwrapped. User code throwing during M-: or eval-region propagates to run-core's catch → full stack trace instead of clean `Eval error: <message>`. Contrast save-buffer which catches cleanly. Fix: try/catch around evaluator.eval/evalExpression; on error `editor.message('Eval error: '+err.message)` and write stack to *Backtrace* scratch buffer.

title: fido RET handler fires while Vertico is the active frontend → wrong candidate accepted
detail: [step-0] fido binds minibuffer return/enter/C-m → icomplete-fido-ret unconditionally at install(). Vertico relies on default exit-minibuffer→minibufferSubmit→frontend.submitValue path. With stephen config (vertico-mode) + fido builtin both loaded, RET fires icomplete-fido-ret, which reads fido's own candidates[selected] index, not Vertico's. User scrolls Vertico to candidate 5, presses RET, gets fido's candidate 0. Fix: Gate fido's RET handler: at top of fidoRet, `if (!editor.globalMinorModes.has('fido-vertical-mode') || editor.minibufferCompletionFrontend) return editor.minibufferSubmit()`. Better: move all defineKey('minibuffer',...) calls into the minor mode's onEnable/onDisable so bindings only exist while fido is the active frontend.

title: Three plugins write the shared minibuffer keymap unconditionally at install — load order owns arrows
detail: [step-1] fido (:182-185), vertico (:93-96), ivy-mode (:72-75) each call defineKey('minibuffer','up'/'down'/'C-n'/'C-p',...) at install() with no mode gating. Single shared map; whichever install() runs last owns the arrows regardless of which minor mode is enabled. Fix: Add `keymap?: Keymap` to MinibufferCompletionFrontend (editor.ts:77-81) and have key dispatch consult `this.minibufferCompletionFrontend?.keymap` before the static minibuffer map. Move each plugin's nav bindings into a per-frontend keymap.

title: Vertico filterCandidates hard-codes startsWith; never consults editor.completer — silently downgrades from flex to prefix
detail: [step-2] filterCandidates hard-codes `candidate.toLowerCase().startsWith(needle)`. Kernel refreshMinibufferCompletions short-circuits to frontend.refresh (1132) before reaching this.completer (1139). Enabling vertico-mode silently downgrades from fido's flex matching to prefix-only even though editor.completer===flexCompleter is still set. Fix: In filterCandidates, take editor as param and `if (editor.completer) return editor.completer(input, candidates)` before startsWith fallback. Lets Vertico provide UI while fido/orderless provides matching style — how real Emacs composes vertico + completion-styles.

title: defvar arrays inferred as type 'string'; customize round-trip corrupts vertico-count-format to literal '['
detail: [step-3] defvar('vertico-count-format', ['%-6s ','%s/%s']) and defvar('vertico-multiline', [...]) store arrays, but defvar infers CustomType via typeof so arrays become 'string'. parseCustomValue for type 'string' returns raw input. M-x customize-variable RET vertico-count-format → display shows JSON, user RET, setCustom stores literal string, then vertico.ts:298 format[0] evaluates to '['. Fix: Extend CustomType to include 'sexp'/'json'; have defvar pick it when typeof value==='object'; have parseCustomValue JSON.parse for that type. Or change vertico's two defvars to scalar strings.

title: customize.ts invents groupForVariable from name-prefix because CustomVariable has no group field
detail: [step-4] groupForVariable takes first hyphen-segment of name as group. transient-mark-mode → 'transient', auto-save-interval → 'auto', icomplete-prospects-height → 'icomplete' (M-x customize-group RET fido shows nothing). Parallel group concept living in UI layer instead of data layer. Fix: Add `group?: string` to CustomVariable and as optional arg to defcustom/defvar. customize.ts:431 becomes `variable.group ?? namePrefixFallback(name)`. Update fido/vertico defvars to pass explicit groups.

title: customize buffer help line advertises r/u/d/g but customize-mode-map binds none; resetCustom never wired
detail: [step-5] Help line at :255 advertises `r reset, u reset-saved, d describe, g refresh` but customize-mode-map (lines 26-37) binds none, installCustomizeCommands defines no reset/describe/refresh commands. runtime/custom.ts exports resetCustom/resetCustomToSaved (71,83) — never called, never tested. runtime-parity.test.ts:215-228 even asserts Custom-reset-* are undefined, pinning the absence of the feature the UI claims exists. Fix: Either bind them — keymap.bind('r','customize-reset-variable') calling resetCustom(variableAtPoint.name)+refreshCustomizeBuffer, 'u'→resetCustomToSaved, 'g'→refreshCustomizeBuffer, 'd'→describe-variable — and add a runtime-parity test (set→reset→assert baseline). Or delete the four phantom keys from the help string at :255.

title: completingReadFunction save/restore-previous pattern footguns with 3 completion plugins (enable-disable order)
detail: [step-6] Vertico onEnable saves editor.completingReadFunction to WeakMap and restores on disable. ivy-mode does same dance on same field. enable-ivy → enable-vertico → disable-ivy → disable-vertico leaves completingReadFunction pointing at ivy's saved value even though ivy is off. Fix: Replace scalar `completingReadFunction: CompletingReadFunction|null` with a stack on Editor: pushCompletingReadFunction(fn)/popCompletingReadFunction(fn) (pop removes by identity, not LIFO). Same for minibufferCompletionFrontend.

title: readCustomValue is dead — customizeSetVariable inlines identical prompt+parse
detail: [step-7] readCustomValue (400-405) is dead; customizeSetVariable inlines the identical prompt+parse at :352. Only reference is the definition itself. Fix: Delete readCustomValue, or replace lines 352-354 with `const value = await readCustomValue(editor, variable); if (value===null) return`.

title: tree-sitter parse is non-incremental: every edit-triggered fontLock miss is a full parse
detail: [Perf-1] `parser.parse(buffer.text)` called with no oldTree. fontLockCache stores spans not the Tree, so tree-sitter's incremental-parse capability is thrown away. On a 5k-line TS file ~10-30ms parse+query per keystroke — single largest per-edit cost. Fix: Keep WeakMap<BufferModel, Parser.Tree>. In treeSitterFontLock: fetch old tree, parser.parse(buffer.text, oldTree), store new tree. Feed oldTree.edit({startIndex,oldEndIndex,newEndIndex,...}) from BufferModel.onTextChange. Then restrict query.captures to visible range so span count is O(screen) not O(file).

title: changed() not coalesced — every keystroke fires 2-4 full buildDisplayModel passes
detail: [Perf-2] editor.run() always emits changed (editor.ts:610), but 47 call sites inside command bodies also emit, and editor.message() emits a third (editor.ts:1253). events.emit awaits listeners serially, so each fires synchronous present() with no batching. Every cost in the perf review is multiplied by 2-4. Fix: In run-core.ts:57, replace direct binding.present() with microtask-coalesced scheduler: `let scheduled=false; editor.events.on('changed',()=>{if(scheduled)return; scheduled=true; queueMicrotask(()=>{scheduled=false; binding.present()})})`. Separately drop `void this.changed('message')` from editor.message() — trailing changed in run() covers it.

title: highlightWithQuery overlap dedup is O(captures²) — 10⁸ comparisons per typed char on a few-k-line file
detail: [Perf-3] For each candidate does chosen.some(...) + chosen.findIndex(...), both linear in chosen. 10k-20k captures on a few-thousand-line JS/TS file → 10⁸ comparisons per cache miss. Fix: Sort candidates by start asc, priority desc; single forward sweep keeping lastEnd per priority bucket — or just emit all captures and let applyTheme's boundary merge handle layering. The covered/overlap pass can be deleted if span priority is encoded as array order.

title: orgDisplayFilter rebuilds collapsed text+offset tables every frame; map() is linear scan called 2× per span
detail: [Perf-6] Invoked every frame for org buffers. With even one fold rebuilds entire collapsed text (O(n) split+join) and bufStart/dispStart tables (O(L)) from scratch on every render — no memo. The returned map(n) does linear `while(i+1<bufStart.length...)` scan, called 2× per font-lock span. 5000 spans × 2000 lines → 20M iterations per keystroke. Fix: Memoize filter result in buffer.locals keyed on (buffer.text, foldedRanges) — invalidate on _splice or setFolded. Replace linear scan in map with binarySearch(bufStart,nn). In build-display-model, only remap spans that intersect the visible region.

title: fontLock cache half-effective: lspSpans/overlaySources run on every call; cache stores spans not tree
detail: [Perf-7] Mode fontLock result is text-keyed so navigation hits, but: lsp.diagnosticSpans(buffer) and overlaySources.flatMap run on every call regardless of cache hit; on hit caller immediately spreads [...editor.fontLock(buffer)] shallow-copying S spans per render per window; cache stores spans not tree so edit can't reuse anything. Fix: Key cache on buffer.textVersion integer bumped in _splice instead of string identity. Cache the merged [...spans,...lspSpans,...overlaySpans], invalidated when textVersion/diagnostics version/overlay-source version changes. Have build-display-model push isearch/region spans via concat only when present.

title: Vertico: 353 LOC, 14 commands, only 4 happy-path tests — cycle/group/scroll/exit-input/save untested
detail: [test-0] Zero tests touch vertico-cycle modular wraparound (off-by-one prone), verticoGroup next/previous-group walk, computeScroll margin clamping, vertico-exit-input (M-RET state.exitInput flag), vertico-save, vertico-first/last, vertico-scroll-up/down. grep for these in test/ returns nothing. Fix: Create test/plugins/vertico.test.ts. Minimum: (a) vertico-cycle=true, 3 candidates, vertico-next from index 2 wraps to -1 then 0; (b) computeScroll count=5 margin=2 20 candidates after verticoGoto(17); (c) two directory groups, vertico-next-group jumps to first of second; (d) vertico-exit-input returns raw input not highlighted candidate; (e) vertico-save populates *Vertico Completions*.

title: displayFilter integration in build-display-model has no test — span remapping start>end untested
detail:
[test-1] The displayFilter integration (filt.map() remap of point/mark/spans) is the only place the filter affects what the user sees. test/plugins/org.test.ts unit-tests orgDisplayFilter in isolation but build-display-model.test.ts (3 tests total) never instantiates a mode with a displayFilter. Line 160 dSpans=spans.map(...) can produce start>end spans for highlights inside a folded region — nothing asserts the renderer tolerates that. Fix: Add test: install org plugin, org-mode buffer with `* H
** child
body
* H2`, fold H, put font-lock span on `body` and point on `child`, call buildDisplayModel, assert (1) leaf body text contains `* H...` and `* H2` consecutive, (2) remapped span is zero-width or filtered, (3) syncPoint maps to end of `* H` line.

title: term-v2 renderTerminal cell→UTF-16 offset conversion untested for CJK/emoji (the case the comment warns about)
detail: [test-2] renderTerminal walks xterm cells with getWidth() and getChars().length||1 to convert cell indices to UTF-16 offsets. Comment at 127 explicitly calls out 'surrogate pairs and wide glyphs make these diverge' — yet term-v2-spans.test.ts feeds only ASCII. If either branch is wrong, span.end is off by N and text.slice no longer equals the coloured text. Fix: Add: (a) writeAsync(xt,'a\x1b[31m日本\x1b[0m b') → assert text==='a日本 b' and text.slice(spans[0].start,spans[0].end)==='日本'; (b) writeAsync(xt,'👋x') then assert point after \x1b[1;3H equals UTF-16 offset 4 not 2.

title: customizeVariableAtPoint backward-walk branch and parseCustomValue boolean/NaN paths untested
detail: [test-4] customizeVariableAtPoint has two branches; every existing test lands on branch 1 (point on Variable: line). The handleKey path `n n s` (next-line ×2 then set, point on Value: line) hits branch 2 — untested. parseCustomValue: boolean accepts nil/false/0/no/off and number throws on NaN; tests only feed 'true' and '7'. Fix: Add test: render 2-variable *Customize* buffer, move point to second variable's `  Value:` line, run customize-set-variable — assert it targets variable #2. Add table test for parseCustomValue: ['nil','off','0'].forEach(v=>expect false); expect(()=>parseCustomValue('number','abc')).toThrow(/Invalid number/).

title: ivy-mode (134 LOC, full completing-read frontend) has zero test references
detail: [test-5] Every directory plugin has a matching *.test.ts except dogfood/term. But ivy-mode.ts (134 LOC, ivy-next/previous/partial-or-done) has literally zero test references. vertico and fido both have coverage; ivy is the third sibling and has none. Reachable via M-x ivy-mode then any minibuffer prompt. Fix: Create test/plugins/ivy-mode.test.ts mirroring fido.test.ts: enable ivy-mode, open collection prompt, assert minibufferCompletionDisplay shows matches, ivy-next advances index, submit returns selected candidate, disabling restores previous completingReadFunction. (Note: Simp-3 says delete ivy-mode entirely — resolve that first.)

title: defgeneric redeclare branch (hot-reload path) — fallback update/preserve untested
detail: [test-6] defgeneric's redeclare branch updates doc/fallback/source on existing entry only when option !== undefined. sexp.test.ts checks dispatch identity survives + methods kept, but never checks (a) new fallback on redeclare actually takes effect, (b) redeclaring with {} doesn't wipe a previously-set fallback. Fix: Extend 'defgeneric is idempotent' test: defgeneric('g',{fallback:()=>1}); assert dispatch returns 1; defgeneric('g',{fallback:()=>2}); assert 2; defgeneric('g',{}); assert still 2.

title: writeRaw microtask-batching contract has no direct unit test (only e2e via term-send-raw)
detail: [test-7] writeRaw's microtask-batching (same-tick bytes coalesce into one pty.write) is the fix for t-9689fb (hello→hlleo). loop-t-9689fb.test.ts tests end-to-end term-send-raw path but never calls writeRaw directly. If someone refactors writeRaw to flush eagerly, the e2e test may still pass depending on scheduling. Fix: Add: const s=makeSession(); writeRaw(s.session,'h'); writeRaw(s.session,'i'); expect(pty.writes).toEqual([]); await Promise.resolve(); expect(pty.writes).toEqual(['hi']).

title: isearch lazy-highlight missing — only the current match is painted
detail: [Feat-0] isearchMatchSpan() returns exactly ONE span (current match, face isearch). Real Emacs paints every other visible match with lazy-highlight face. All search primitives (findForward/findRegexpForward/isearchNoUpperCaseP) and overlay-source mechanism already exist. Fix: Add isearchLazyHighlightSpans(buffer,state):TextSpan[] looping findForward from 0, skipping span at buffer.point, returning {start,end,face:'lazyHighlight'}. Add 'lazyHighlight' to FaceName. In build-display-model.ts:150 push ...isearchLazyHighlightSpans alongside current-match span. ~25 lines.

title: isearch C-w (yank-word-or-char) missing — single most-used isearch power move
detail: [Feat-1] handleIsearchKey handles only backspace/return/delete/printable; any modified key falls through and exits isearch. C-w to grab word-at-point into search string is search-for-symbol-under-cursor. buffer.wordForward()/word-regexp machinery already exists. Fix: In handleIsearchKey, before default printable check: if (key.ctrl && key.name==='w') { grab buf.text.slice(buf.point+len, buf.wordEnd(buf.point+len)); setIsearchString(string+that); return {status:'inserted'} }. Same pattern adds C-y and M-s . ~10 lines each.

title: isearch has no search-ring: C-s C-s with empty string does nothing (no recall last search)
detail: [Feat-2] isearchRepeat() returns early at `if(!state?.string)`. In Emacs, C-s RET ... later C-s C-s recalls the last search — fundamental muscle memory. No searchRing/lastIsearchString field exists; endIsearch() discards state.string without saving. Fix: Add `private searchRing: string[] = []` on Editor. In endIsearch() push string if non-empty. In isearchRepeat(): `if(!state.string){state.string=this.searchRing.at(-1)??''; if(!state.string)return}` then fall through. Also enables wrap-around. ~15 lines.

title: magit-status/magit-log have NO diff font-lock — +/-/@@ lines render monochrome
detail: [Feat-3] defineMode({name:'magit-status', parent:'magit-special', keymap}) passes no fontLock. Inline +added/-removed/@@ hunk lines from buildStatus() render plain. magit's entire visual value is the colored diff. parseDiff already produces hunk structure; FaceName has string/error/builtin/comment to repurpose; diredFontLock shows the line-scan pattern. Fix: Add magitDiffFontLock(buffer):TextSpan[] that walks lines: ^@@→builtin, ^+→string (or new diffAdded), ^-→error (or diffRemoved), ^(Head|Merge|Unstaged|Staged|Recent)→keyword. Pass as fontLock to magit-status, magit-log, magit-revision defineMode calls. ~20 lines.

title: magit-log RET opens git show as mode 'text' in same window — no q-to-bury, no coloring, lose place in log
detail: [Feat-4] magit-log-show-commit opens git show output as mode 'text' in the SAME window. No q to bury (text doesn't inherit magit-special), no diff coloring, can't n/p through commits while watching diffs scroll. Split-and-select-back pattern already exists 80 lines above in magit-commit. Fix: Define magit-revision mode (parent magit-special, fontLock:magitDiffFontLock). In magit-log-show-commit: const logWin=editor.selectedWindowId; editor.splitWindowBelow(); editor.scratch(name,out,'magit-revision'); editor.selectWindow(logWin). Now n/RET/n/RET works like real magit. ~15 lines.

title: wdired makes ENTIRE buffer writable — stray edit past col 22 corrupts prefix → file not renamed
detail: [Feat-5] Nothing protects columns 0..NAME_OFFSET (mark/type/size/date). Real wdired puts read-only text-properties on prefix. A stray C-a C-k or backspace past col 22 corrupts the prefix; relocateByPrefix() can't find unique match → marker dropped → 'N line(s) ignored' with no clue why. The onTextChange hook is ALREADY installed at :136. Fix: In the onTextChange wrapper at :136, before adjustMarkers, check if edit crosses into a prefix → editor.message('Read-only: edit the filename column only'). Can't veto the splice, so additionally restore m.prefix into the line on wdired-finish-edit before reading nameAt(). Simpler 80% fix: re-derive nameAt by stripping /^.{22}/ per line. ~12 lines.

title: RegisterContents has no {kind:'text'} — C-x r s / C-x r i (most common register use) unimplementable
detail: [Feat-6] Union has only point and window-configuration. copy-to-register / insert-register, the most common register use, are unimplementable. Registers 60% built (storage map, point-jump, winconf-restore); rectangle commands ARE implemented so the C-x r prefix is wired. Fix: Add `| {kind:'text'; text:string}` to union. In core/commands.ts add copy-to-register (read region→store) and insert-register (lookup→buffer.insert). Bind C-x r s / C-x r i. ~20 lines. Bonus: |{kind:'rectangle'; lines:string[]}.

title: query-replace emits NO highlight on the match being prompted for — confirming a replacement you can't see
detail: [Feat-7] query-replace has full y/n/!/./^/q state machine but `buffer.point=at` moves cursor but you can't see match extent. Real Emacs paints current match with query-replace face + lazy-highlights the rest. editor.addOverlaySource hook (used by avy) is exactly the mechanism. Fix: Before while loop: let current=[]; editor.addOverlaySource(b=>b.id===buffer.id?current:[]). Inside loop after buffer.point=at: current=[{start:at,end:at+from.length,face:'isearch'}]; await editor.changed(...). After loop: current=[]. ~8 lines.

title: counsel-ag in core/commands.ts is shadowed by plugins/next-error — 17 dead lines, inferior copy
detail: [Simp-0] Registered here AND at plugins/next-error/index.ts:147. next-error is in builtin.ts (loads after core) so this 17-line body never executes. Uses process.cwd() not findProjectRoot, doesn't setLocationList, doesn't set default-directory/next-error-locations locals. Fix: Delete commands.ts:626-642. grep/rgrep alias survives via plugins/fido/index.ts:177-179.

title: Dead revert-buffer in emacs-standard.ts — commands.ts:732 is the canonical override
detail: [Simp-1] commands.ts:732 documents the override. This older version reimplements via readFileText+setText instead of buffer.revert() (skips mtime tracking, no noconfirm arg). Pure noise — 15 lines that look load-bearing but aren't. Fix: Delete emacs-standard.ts:168-182 and the readFileText import if unused. Move canonical definition from commands.ts:733 into emacs-standard.ts so there's exactly one.

title: term v1's install() is fully shadowed — v2 re-registers all 7 commands; ~70 dead lines per startup
detail: [Simp-2] builtin.ts loads term then term-v2; v2 re-registers all 7 commands against its own sessions WeakMap, re-defines term mode, re-populates term-map keymap with byte-identical loop. The 'reuse v1 keymap' comment is fictional — v2 repopulates anyway. Only keyToPtyBytes/termRawMap/pty.ts actually reused. Fix: Gut plugins/term/index.ts to export only keyToPtyBytes/TermRawMap (rename to key-encoding.ts or fold into term-v2). Delete ['term',...] from builtin.ts:33. In term-v2:188 drop the getMode('term')?.keymap?? guard. Update the 8 test/bugs/loop-t-*.test.ts files.

title: ivy-mode: third parallel completion frontend, zero references — delete
detail: [Simp-3] fido is builtin, vertico loaded by config/stephen.ts, ivy-mode loaded by nothing — not in builtin.ts, no test, no config imports it. 134 lines of WeakMap state + *ivy-completions* renderer that duplicates fido/vertico via MinibufferCompletionFrontend. Fix: Delete plugins/ivy-mode.ts. If someone wants ivy-style horizontal candidates later, that's a defcustom on fido. (Conflicts with test-5 which says add tests; deletion is preferred.)

title: project-compile is a degraded reimplementation of compile — no streaming, no error parsing, wrong mode
detail: [Simp-4] Blocks on full process output before showing anything (no streaming), doesn't parse error locations, doesn't populate next-error's location list, no recompile/kill-compilation, sets mode to 'text' with kind='grep' instead of 'compilation'. compile's compilationStart() already does cwd-override + streaming + parsing + setLocationList. Fix: Replace body of project-compile (108-131) with: compute root, prompt for cmd, `await compilationStart(editor, cmd, root)`. Import from ../compile. ~28 lines → ~5. Delete redundant defcustom('compile-command') at :65.

title: projectRoot() duplicates findProjectRoot() with worse semantics (only checks .git)
detail: [Simp-5] Both walk parents for a marker; project's only checks .git, lsp's checks 8 markers (Cargo.toml, package.json, pyproject.toml, …). magit imports projectRoot from ../project; compile/next-error/lsp-monorepo import findProjectRoot from lsp. Two root-finders → 'compile runs in dir X but project-find-file lists dir Y'. Fix: Delete projectRoot (project/index.ts:8-16). Re-export findProjectRoot from lsp/project-root or import directly in project+magit. One signature wrinkle: findProjectRoot takes a file path and never returns null; project's took a dir and returned string|null — callers need a one-line adapter.

title: fzf-git is project-find-file done worse (process.cwd, no -z, .includes filter)
detail: [Simp-6] Runs `git ls-files` in process.cwd() (wrong when cwd≠repo root), no -z so newlines break split, filters with .includes(query) instead of flex completer, doesn't rememberProject. plugins/project/index.ts:74-89 is canonical and bound to C-x p f / C-x C-z. Fix: Delete commands.ts:617-624. If fzf-git name is muscle-memory, add `editor.command('fzf-git', ctx => editor.run('project-find-file', ctx.args))` in plugins/project.

title: set-mark-command + isearch-forward/backward in core are shadowed by builtin plugins (mark-ring, isearch-regexp)
detail: [Simp-7] set-mark-command (156-159) and isearch-forward/backward (182-190) immediately overwritten by builtin plugins: mark-ring re-registers with full ring + C-u pop; isearch-regexp re-registers with smart case-fold. Core stubs never execute. Same shadowing class as counsel-ag/revert-buffer. Fix: Delete commands.ts:156-159 and 182-190 (~13 lines). No behavior change. While there, audit editor.command calls in core/commands.ts against grep -c 'editor.command' plugins/ for other shadowed names.

title: lineStarts consumers: viewport.ts visibleTextRegionFromStart
detail:
Use buffer.lineStarts/lineAt/lineBounds instead of split("
"). The primitive is in buffer.ts.

title: lineStarts consumers: lsp/positions.ts positionToPoint/pointToPosition
detail:
Use buffer.lineStarts/lineAt/lineBounds instead of split("
"). The primitive is in buffer.ts.

title: lineStarts consumers: line-numbers.ts firstVisibleLineNumber/mapVisibleOffset
detail:
Use buffer.lineStarts/lineAt/lineBounds instead of split("
"). The primitive is in buffer.ts.

title: isearch: RET inserts a newline into the buffer instead of exiting search
detail: tui-drive: C-s delta RET → buffer gains a blank line at point and is marked modified; echo area still shows stale 'I-search: delta'. Root cause: handleIsearchKey (src/kernel/editor.ts:1008) switches on key.name and matches case 'return', but keyEventFromOpentui→canonicalizeKeyEvent→normalizeKeyName has already aliased 'return'→'enter' (KEY_NAME_ALIASES, src/kernel/keymap.ts:175), so the case never fires. RET falls through to keymaps.feed → 'newline', which self-inserts, then the finally-block ends isearch. Fix: match 'enter' (or both) in handleIsearchKey.

title: isearch: M-p/M-n fall through to flymake instead of recalling search-ring
detail: tui-drive: C-s delta RET, M-< C-s M-p → echo 'No more Flymake diagnostics', isearch ended. be88a61 added Editor.searchRing and isearchRepeat() reads it for C-s C-s (works), but M-p/M-n have no arm in handleIsearchKey, so they reach the global keymap where flymake-nav binds M-p/M-n. Commit message claims 'search-ring recall' — only the C-s C-s path is wired. Fix: add M-p/M-n arms in handleIsearchKey that walk searchRing and call setIsearchString.

title: register-text: orphaned comment describes a jump-to-register override that no longer exists
detail: c179c03 deleted the jump-to-register re-registration from plugins/register-text/index.ts but left the 3-line comment ('Core jump-to-register predates text/rectangle kinds … re-register so non-location registers are refused'). The comment now documents code that isn't there and contradicts current core behavior (f6aa2dc added the kind check). Delete the comment.

title: isearch lazy-highlight: spans emitted but no theme entry — other matches render invisibly
detail: tui-drive: open file with many "a"s, C-s a, capansi → only the current match has bg #6a5f00; every other "a" is plain fg. be88a61 added isearchLazyHighlightSpans() (src/kernel/isearch.ts:117) emitting face "lazyHighlight", wired it into build-display-model.ts:151, and added "lazyHighlight" to FaceName (src/modes/mode.ts:21) — but src/themes/jemacs-dark.ts has only `isearch:` (line 16), no `lazyHighlight:` entry. With no FaceStyle the spans fall through to default colors. Fix: add `lazyHighlight: { bg: "#3a3a5a" }` (or similar dimmer-than-isearch bg) to src/themes/jemacs-dark.ts.

title: editor.addHook deleted (c3826d9) but README + definitions.ts still reference it — documented user API now throws
detail: tui-drive: M-x eval-expression, editor.addHook('find-file-hook',()=>{}) → *Backtrace* with 'TypeError: editor.addHook is not a function'. c3826d9 (t-b1c3fe99) deleted Editor.addHook/removeHook and migrated 14 internal callers to import {addHook} from kernel/hooks, but: (a) README.md:104 still documents editor.addHook(...) as the hook surface in the find-definition table — any ~/.jemacs/init.ts following the README now throws at boot; (b) src/runtime/definitions.ts:69 classifyForm regex still matches /editor\.addHook/ so M-x find-definition would source-track a form that can never run. The eval runtime already exports bare addHook (src/runtime/jemacs-runtime.ts:8) and that form works. Fix: update README.md:104 to show addHook(...) instead of editor.addHook(...), and change the definitions.ts regex to /\baddHook\s*\(/ (drop the editor. prefix).

title: C-x C-f completion is case-sensitive (should fold)
detail: Type "readme" → no match for README.md. fido flexCompleter / file completion should case-fold like Emacs read-file-name-completion-ignore-case=t. Match against .toLowerCase() but display original case.

title: M-> sometimes scrolls past end of screen
detail: After end-of-buffer, point is at text.length but the viewport sometimes shows it below the window (point off-screen). syncSelectedWindowViewport may not be called after the command, or its math is off when point is exactly at EOF on a trailing newline. Repro via test/tui.
